Protection and control device

PowerLogic P7 overview

PowerLogic P7 overview
PowerLogic P7 range is a platform of digital protection and control devices for
modern Power Systems that provides hardware and firmware flexibility. This
allows selection of hardware modules and firmware application levels to meet
specific requirements. The product selection includes choice of required hardware
modules and accessories like analog boards, input/output modules, Ethernet ports
and so on. Application packages and corresponding application levels can be
selected from a comprehensive list.
PowerLogic P7 devices are expandable and support additional hardware and
functional requirements during installation or future system expansion. A license
upgrade is required depending upon what functionality is required to be added.
PowerLogic P7 platform provides wide range of functionality supported by the
engineering tool, PowerLogic Engineering Suite. This is a versatile tool that guides
the user through the lifecycle of the product from design up to operation.
PowerLogic P7 devices can be applied in a wide range of electrical domains.
Utilities

Industry

Buildings

Generation
Distribution
Transmission

Oil and Gas
Mining, Mineral and Metals
Electro-intensive

Data Centers

Properties of PowerLogic P7
PowerLogic P7 platform is built on technology concepts developed in close
cooperation with customers to help ensure the safety of the Power Systems.
PowerLogic P7 devices can be tailored to suit customer applications over the
entire product life cycle. Ease of use, hardware modularity, and firmware flexibility
allows PowerLogic P7 to be upgraded or modified as application requirements
evolve.

Features
•

Scalable hardware and firmware platform.

•

Flexible firmware and modular hardware with extensive protection
functionality and a 7-inch color touchscreen HMI.

•

Digital substation ready with IEC 61850 Edition 2.1.

•

EcoStruxure™ connected product for asset management and condition
monitoring.

•

Cybersecurity compliance with IEC 62443-4-2 Security Level 2.

Hardware modularity
The modular and expandable structure of PowerLogic P7 supports various
configuration options to suit the application throughout the product lifecycle.

22

•

The 40TE case has nine slots, including three fixed slots and six user
selectable slots. It provides increased reliability and high operational safety
with a design that meets extreme environmental conditions.

•

Fixed slots are fitted with the processor/communication module, power supply
module, and auxiliary power supply module respectively.

•

User selectable slots support flexible allocation of various input modules.

P7/EN M/02-004A

PowerLogic P7 overview

Protection and control device

•

The hardware design supports flexible configuration of hardware features and
layout. For supported hardware combinations, see Order information, page
624. For hardware configuration editing, see Editing device hardware
configuration, page 161.

•

Standardized analog input modules, binary I/O modules, communication
modules, and accessories across the entire range facilitate replacement and
minimize spare stock.

•

Precision Time Protocol (PTP) time synchronization is available as standard.

•

Inter-Range Instrumentation Group-B (IRIG-B) time synchronization is
supported through an external IRIG-B module connected to the extension
port.

•

Temperature measurement (RTD) modules and Current Loop Input/Output
(CLIO) modules, are supported through the extension port.

•

Various Ethernet Small Form-factor Pluggable (SFP) modules, available as
accessories, enhance flexibility for demanding Ethernet architectures.

Firmware flexibility
PowerLogic P7 devices are capable of adapting to customized device
functionality. An approach towards flexibility through different licensing levels
allows the user to select an application level and benefit from the functionality
offered by the corresponding level.
Within the chosen application level, you can freely configure the available
functions and features in the corresponding application templates. For details
about the available functions and features that can be configured with product
flexibility, see Function table, page 27.
•

•

The main application selected defines the protection functions available in the
application template.
◦

B – Busbar

◦

C – Bay controller

◦

F – Feeder

◦

G – Generator

◦

L – Line differential

◦

M – Motor

◦

S – Stand-alone Merging Unit (SAMU)

◦

T – Transformer

The application level defines the available functions in a bay depending on
the main application.
◦

Essential level applications (Level 0)

◦

Advanced level applications (Level 1)

◦

Advanced+ level applications (Level 2)
NOTE: Main application can be changed and application level can be
increased with PowerLogic Engineering Suite. See License update, page
613 for details.

P7/EN M/02-004A

23

Protection and control device

PowerLogic P7 overview

•

Bay is used to group equipment, functions, measurements, and records used
by the application. Equipment and functions can be added with minimal user
configuration and include default signal routing for operation. Signal routing
includes routing of analog signals like single and three phase currents and
voltages to the corresponding analog modules, binary signals like Trip, trigger
for Disturbance recorder.
◦

Current Transformer (CT) and Voltage Transformer (VT) sources are part
of a bay which are routed to physical CT inputs in accordance with the
selected user analog modules. A single CT and VT source are defined as
the bay measurements used for load monitoring. This CT source is also
used as the reference for 3ph VT supervision.

◦

Circuit breakers and switches can be added to a bay. Associated functions
such as interlocking and breaker failure are automatically associated.
Optional functions such as synch check can also be associated depending
on the requirement and main application level.

◦

Protection and control functions are available depending on the main
application and license level. Most functions will operate based on the bay
measurements while functions such as differential will require additional
sources.
Some simple functions such as current and voltage protection allow the
source CT or VT to be changed from the default bay measurement to a
different source as required by the application.

◦

Bay measurements depend on the main application and installed
functions. For example, adding motor thermal protection function will add
thermal to the bay measurements.

◦

Records (Fault and Disturbance) are automatically created for the bay.
These records include any additional equipment or function signals that
are added to the bay.

Figure 1 - Example of bay representation for motor protection

V

Bay measurement
Bay CT/VT

I

Protection
Trip

M

Fault record

DR

I
Differential CT
P71112A

Protection

Includes protection functions based on bay measurements and functions
based on other CTs and VTs connected to the bay.

Bay measurement

Includes load measurements (Hz, W, Wh) from the bay CT and VT.

Color graphical user interface (order option)

24

•

7-inch color touchscreen display with mimic view.

•

Home and Reset physical buttons, and four physical LEDs indicating the
status of power supply, trip, alarm and out of service.

P7/EN M/02-004A

PowerLogic P7 overview

Protection and control device

•

24 user configurable virtual LEDs and 12 function keys on the touchscreen.

•

Customizable user displays for efficient viewing of data through graph or
meters, standard control, annunciations.

•

USB (Type B, Mini) interface for access to PowerLogic Engineering Suite or
the web HMI.

Substation communication
•

The processor module includes an integrated single Ethernet RJ45 port that
can be used for engineering access and/or Supervisory Control And Data
Acquisition (SCADA).

•

Additional redundant interface through SFP ports (RJ45, Fiber Optic (FO)
Multimode, FO Singlemode) for Ethernet architectures supporting
redundancy protocols such as High availability Seamless Redundancy
(HSR), Parallel Redundancy Protocol (PRP), Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol
(RSTP) and Failover. A single SFP can also be used as an additional non
redundant interface.

•

Time synchronization through Ethernet using Simple Network Time Protocol
(SNTP) or IEEE 1588 PTP is supported. IRIG-B time synchronization is
supported via an external module using the extension port. A PTP boundary
clock can be enabled on the redundant ports to act as a master for devices on
that network such as merging units.

•

IEC 61850 Edition 2.1, Distributed Network Protocol 3 (DNP3) and Modbus
server (slave) communication protocols through any of the Ethernet ports.

•

DNP3, Modbus server and IEC 60870-5-103 protocols through an RS485
serial port.

•

Additional Internet Protocols (IPs) and Virtual Local Area Networks (VLANs)
can be added for Local Area Network (LAN) segregation.

•

VLAN and out of band management (port hardening for different protocols)
parameters on single Ethernet RJ45 port and redundant SFP ports are
configurable.

Virtualized protection and control
PowerLogic P7 also provides a virtualized protection and control solution. The
virtual P7 provides the same protection and control functionality as the physical
device, subject to the selected license. It is implemented as software running on
virtualization environment hosted on server-based hardware. This decouples the
protection and control application from dedicated IED hardware.
The server-based hardware (Head Unit, HU) runs the virtualization layer
(hypervisor) that hosts the virtual machines (VMs) and provides the physical
network interfaces used for process bus and station bus communications. In the
VM, each virtual IED runs in an isolated container, allowing multiple virtual IEDs to
run on a single HU server platform.
In this virtualized solution, merging units (MUs) provide the process interface for
the virtual IED. The MUs digitize the analog signals from CTs/VTs and publish
sampled values (SV) streams over the process bus. The virtual IED subscribes to
these SV streams and exchange GOOSE messages for signaling or tripping, with
performance equivalent to the physical device.
The virtual P7 is not available for direct order. Contact Schneider Electric for
information about the virtualized offer.

Function table
The function table gives an overview of product flexibility for different applications
and the routing information for each function.

Product flexibility
Product flexibility allows flexible functionality of the IED. The functions can be
selected and loaded into the IED. The functionality is managed by assignment of
flex points and is subject to a flex point budget.
The PowerLogic P7 device is pre-configured with basic functionality by default.
Device functionality can be extended by adding functions and stages available in
the application-specific function library. The additional functionality is subject to
the flex point budget for the selected application level and to the stage limit of
each function. The flex point budget for each application level are:
•

Essential (L0): Up to 500 flex points.

•

Advanced (L1): Up to 1000 flex points.

•

Advanced+ (L2): Up to 1000 flex points for physical P7 and up to 2000 flex
points for virtual P7.

The flex point for each function varies by the weight of the function. Every function
type enabled will use the allocated points. Every stage will also use the allocated
points. This means the first stage of any function will consume twice the points of
the subsequent stages. The functions not allowing flexibility configuration are fixed
and do not affect the flex points.
The flexibility configurability as well as the maximum number of stages for each
function are listed in PowerLogic P7 function table for main applications, page 29.
To modify the functions and stages of your device with PowerLogic Engineering
Suite, see Editing device capabilities, page 162.
To upgrade the application level to extend flexibility of your PowerLogic P7,
contact your local Schneider Electric sales representative. See License update,
page 613.

P7/EN M/02-004A

27

Protection and control device

PowerLogic P7 overview

Routing information
Routing information is about the configurability of the CT/VT source or the analog
inputs of the function. In PowerLogic P7 function table for main applications, page
29:
•

Bay: Bay CT/VT routing, which means the CT/VT source of the function is
fixed to the bay. Bay CT/VT routing can be configured with PowerLogic
Engineering Suite. For details about bay routing, see Bay routing, page 471.

•

Routable: Functional routing, which means the CT/VT source of the
function can be modified.

•

Flexible: Flexible analog routing, which means the analog measurement
inputs to the function can be flexibly selected.
NOTE: To avoid an invalid configuration, not all connections can be modified.
When the default functional routing of PowerLogic P7 application or the bay
routing for the bay function is modified, verify that the behavior is logical by
testing the application.

P7/EN M/02-004A

Table 2 - PowerLogic P7 function table for main applications (Continued)

NOTE:
(1) Flex config = Flexible configuration. Yes means the function and stages can be flexibly added or removed. Fixed means the function cannot be added or removed, and its stages cannot be edited.
(2) ■ means the function is available for the application. – means the function is not available for the application. Number marked with * means a minimum of one stage is required for this function.
(3) This comes from the SCD.
(4) This function is not available for virtual P7.
(5) This application is not available for virtual P7.

(9)

A minimum threshold of 0.025 pu (standard CT) or 0.005 pu (CBCT) is recommended in applications where a magnetic field of 1000 A/m
is possible. Lower settings may be used if required; however, a trip delay of at least 5 s will be required to avoid risk for false tripping
during a heavy magnetic field.
(10) It is recommended to use operation time of at least 30 ms at the lowest pickup value setting.
(11) When digital inputs are used, it is recommended to set the filter time to 20 ms.

44

P7/EN M/02-004A

Installation

Protection and control device

Installation
Safety instructions
This page contains important safety instructions that must be followed precisely
before attempting to install, repair, service or maintain electrical equipment.
Carefully read and follow the safety instructions described below. Only qualified
personnel, equipped with appropriate personal protection equipment, may work
on or operate the equipment. Qualified personnel are individuals who are:
•

familiar with the installation, commissioning, and operation of the equipment
and of the system to which it is being connected.

•

able to safely perform switching operations in accordance with accepted
safety engineering practices and are authorized to energize and de-energize
equipment and to isolate, ground, and label it.

•

trained in the care and use of safety apparatus in accordance with safety
engineering practices.

•

trained in emergency procedures (first aid).

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Turn off all power supplying the protection and control device and the
equipment in which it is installed before working on it.

•

Verify that all power is off using a properly rated voltage sensing device.

•

Replace all devices, doors and covers before turning on power to this
equipment.

•

Apply appropriate personal protective equipment and follow safe electrical
work practices. See local regulation.

•

Do not install this product in ATEX class 0, 1 and 2 areas unless certified for
this application.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
•

Apply proper tightening torque to all wire connections.

•

Disconnect the power supply before removing or replacing a module.

•

Never touch electronic parts.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
Do not install fuses on current transformer (CT) circuits since opening a CT
circuit can produce hazardous voltages.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

P7/EN M/02-004A

45

Protection and control device

Installation

WARNING
UNINTENDED EQUIPMENT OPERATION
Do not energize the primary circuit before the protection device is properly
configured.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or
equipment damage.

CAUTION
HEAVY OBJECT
Lift the device with care.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in injury or equipment
damage.

Protection Class I equipment
Before energizing the equipment, it must be grounded using the protective
conductor terminal.
The protective conductor (ground) connection must not be removed as the
protection against electric shock provided by the equipment would be lost.
When the protective (ground) conductor terminal (PCT) is also used to terminate
cable screens, etc., it is essential that the integrity of the protective (ground)
conductor is checked after the addition or removal of such functional ground
connections. For M4 stud PCTs, the integrity of the protective (ground)
connections should be ensured by using of a locknut or similar.
The recommended minimum protective conductor (ground) wire size is 2.5 mm² or
AWG 12 unless otherwise stated in the technical data section of the equipment
documentation, or required by local or country wiring regulations.
The protective conductor (ground) connection must be low-inductance and as
short as possible.
All connections to the equipment must have a defined potential. Connections that
are pre-wired, but not used, should preferably be grounded when binary inputs
and output relays are isolated. When binary inputs and output relays are
connected to common potential, the pre-wired but unused connections should be
connected to the common potential of the grouped connections.

UL and CSA/CUL Listed or Recognized equipment
To maintain UL and CSA/CUL Listing/Recognized status for North America, the
equipment should be installed using UL or CSA Listed or Recognized parts for the
following items: connection cables, protective fuses/fuseholders or circuit
breakers, insulation crimp terminals and replacement internal battery, as specified
in the equipment documentation.
For external protective fuses, a UL or CSA Listed fuse shall be used. The Listed
type shall be a Class J time delay fuse, with a maximum current rating of 15 A and
a minimum DC/AC rating of 250 V DC/AC, for example, type AJT15.
Where UL or CSA Listing of the equipment is not required, a high rupture capacity
(HRC) fuse type with a maximum current rating of 16 A and a minimum DC/AC
rating of 250 V DC/AC may be used, for example, Red Spot type NIT or TIA.
The corresponding certified Miniature Circuit Breaker (MCB), type B, rated 10 A
for AC or 6 A for DC, is recommended.

46

P7/EN M/02-004A

Installation

Protection and control device

Transport, handling, storage and unpacking
Transport
In its original packing, a PowerLogic P7 can be shipped to any destination by all
usual means of transport.
If installed in a cubicle, a PowerLogic P7 can be transported by all usual means of
transport in the customary conditions used for cubicles. Storage conditions should
be taken into consideration for a long period of transport.

Handling
PowerLogic P7 requires careful handling.
Handle it in the original packing to protect it from damage.
Check the condition of the device by visual inspection and energizing if it is
installed in a cubicle.

Storage

NOTICE
WATER DAMAGE
•

Do not expose the products to sustained humidity during storage.

•

Electrically energize the products within three months of unpacking.

•

Where electrical equipment is being installed, allow sufficient time for
acclimatization to the ambient temperature of the environment before
powering on.

•

Supply power to the protection device every two years for at least one hour.

Failure to follow these instructions can reduce the product life span.
If the PowerLogic P7 device is not to be installed immediately after receipt, store it
in its original packaging in a place free from dust, humidity, and moisture.
For long-term storage, the device can be stored in its original packaging under
storage conditions that meet IEC 60721-3-1, Class 1K23.
Long-term storage temperature: -40℃ to 70℃ (-40℉ to 158℉)
Short-duration exposure outside this range is limited to the values and duration
specified in Environmental characteristics, page 40.
When unpacking the device, make sure that any dust collected on the carton does
not fall inside the device. In locations of high humidity, the carton and packing
materials may become impregnated with moisture that the de-humidifier crystals
will lose their efficiency.
It is recommended to check the storage environment and the packaging condition
annually.
It is recommended to power on the device for one hour every two years.
After unpacking the device, it is recommended to energize the device as soon as
possible in an environment with appropriate temperature, humidity and pollution
levels.
If the device is installed in a cubicle, keep the cubicle protective packing in place
as long as possible. Do not store the cubicle in a damp environment for more than

P7/EN M/02-004A

47

Protection and control device

Installation

a month. The cubicle should be energized as soon as possible. If this is not
possible, the cubicle reheating system should be activated.

Unpacking
Equipment receipt

NOTICE
PRODUCT TAMPERING
Our products leave our factory in closed, sealed original packaging. At delivery,
if the packaging is opened or the seal is broken, the confidentiality and
authenticity of the information contained in the products cannot be ensured.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in compromised
confidentiality and authenticity of the information contained in the
products.
PowerLogic P7 is shipped in a cardboard box which helps protect it against the
shocks received in transport.
If a PowerLogic P7 device is not immediately installed after receipt, it should be
stored in its original packaging in a dry, dust-free area.

Package contents
Each PowerLogic P7 is delivered in an independent package containing:
•

A PowerLogic P7 (max. weight: 9.7 kg/21.3 lb) ①
NOTE: The availability of HMI depends on the order option.

•

A certificate of conformity ②

•

An instruction sheet ③ providing main information about installation and use

•

A top accessory box ④ including the following:
◦

4-teminal serial plug

◦

CT/VT screws (×20 per terminal block)(12)

◦

CT/VT jumpers (×3 per terminal block)(13)

◦

Mounting screws (M4 self-tapping screws ×5)

◦

Slot label

◦

Optional Ethernet (SFP) module(s)
NOTE: Slot label stickers can be attached to terminal blocks to identify
the corresponding slots. This helps prevent incorrect assembly during
device or module replacement.

•

•

A bottom accessory box ⑤ including the following:
◦

IP20 cover for CT/VT(12)

◦

Barrier

A wall mounting adapter plate (including countersunk screws ×5) ⑥
NOTE: The availability of wall mounting adapter plate depends on the
order option.

(12) Only included when the device is ordered with CT/VT ring-type terminals.
(13) Jumper type depends on the CT/VT terminal type (ring-type or pin-type).

Turn off all power supplying the protection and control device and the
equipment in which it is installed before working on it.

is off.

work practices. See local regulation.

equipment.

for this application.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

RISQUE DE CHOC ELECTRIQUE, D'EXPLOSION OU D'ARC
Coupez toutes les alimentations électriques du dispositif de protection et de
contrôle et des équipements dans lesquels il est installé avant d'y intervenir.
Utilisez toujours un vérificateur d'absence de tension (VAT) de tension
correctement assignée pour confirmer que l'alimentation est coupée.

pratiques de travail électrique sécuritaires.

cet équipement sous tension.

qu'il soit certifié pour cette application.
Le non-respect de ces instructions entraînera la mort ou des blessures

P711B8C

Operating environment
Operating temperature and humidity

NOTICE
POTENTIAL DAMAGE FROM ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS
The device must be installed in an environment within the specified operation
temperature and humidity.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.
PowerLogic P7 is intended for indoor installation and use only. If it is required for
use in an outdoor environment, it must be mounted in a specific cabinet. The USB
door must be closed in both cases, which qualifies the device to meet the
requirements of IEC 60529 with the classification of protection degree IP54 (dust
and splashing water protected).
The temperature/relative humidity factors must be compatible with the
environmental withstand characteristics of PowerLogic P7:

50

•

Recommended operating range of temperature: -10...55℃ (14...131℉).

•

Maximum operating temperature: -40...70℃ (-40...158℉).

•

Average humidity: < 75% RH over the year.

•

Temporary permissible humidity: < 93% RH (less than 80 hours per year)

•

No condensation.

P7/EN M/02-004A

Installation

Protection and control device

If the operating conditions are outside the normal range, special arrangements
should be made before commissioning, such as air conditioning of the premises.

Operation in a polluted atmosphere
A contaminated industrial atmosphere (such as the presence of chlorine,
hydrofluoric acid, sulphur, solvents, etc.) can cause corrosion of the electronic
components and terminals, in which case environmental control arrangements
should be made (such as pressurized premises with filtered air, etc.) before
commissioning.
To improve robustness of PowerLogic P7 devices against such environment, all
the electronic boards including CPU have a conformal coating.

Flush mounting

CAUTION
CUTS AND PHYSICAL IMPACT
•

Wear gloves and safety shoes.

•

Trim the edge of the panel cut-out to remove any jagged edges.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in injury or equipment
damage.

PowerLogic P7

Slot: Module and port

✔

A: CPU
•

① EXT: CAN bus port for connection with additional accessories like
IRIG-B module, RTD module, CLIO module.

•

② Port 1: Ethernet port for single RJ45

•

③ Port 2: Ethernet port for optional SFP with HSR/PRP/RSTP/
Failover redundancy

•

④ Port 3: Ethernet port for optional SFP with HSR/PRP/RSTP/
Failover redundancy

•

⑤ Serial port: 2-wire RS485

•

⑥ DHMI port (only available on the no HMI option): for connection
with a detachable HMI, PowerLogic Engineering Suite, or web HMI.

Optional(14)

B-C/D-E/F-G (2 slots): CT/VT analog input module (6CT + 3VT or 5CT +
4VT).

Optional

B/C/D/E/F/G(15): MIO (mixed binary input/output module), DMC (direct
motor control module), or INP (binary input module).

✔

Y: TIO (auxiliary power supply unit)

✔

Z: PSU (power supply unit)

Typical application diagrams
The following sections describe typical application diagrams.

60

P7/EN M/02-004A

Installation

Protection and control device

NOTE: Due to the flexible hardware configuration of PowerLogic P7, the slot
number and terminal number of the binary I/O modules in the following
diagrams are just for an example. The actual number should be based on the
location of other modules such as CT/VT, I/O.

CT typical application
The current input physical channels are mapped to the bays and subsequent
protection functions using CT groups.
These groups can be configured to suit the application needs. The number and
type available depend on the ordered application level of the product.
In the example below, the first application shown in Three phase and ground
connection, page 61, one three-phase CT group and one single phase CT group
are used with the three-phase CT group set to 3ph and the single phase group set
to IN.
A similar mechanism is used for VT's. Refer to CT group, page 442 and VT group,
page 448 for more mapping details.

Direct LV connection (up to 480 V)
Low-voltage systems (up to 480 V) can be directly connected to the voltage input
physical channels without VTs using phase to neutral connections.
The voltage input physical channels are mapped to the bays and subsequent
protection functions using VT groups.
These groups can be configured to suit the application needs. The number and
type available depend on the application level available to the product.
Refer to VT group, page 448 for more mapping details.

Contactor application
Contactor-type switching devices need to be held in closed state by a normally
closed (NC) contact which is then opened for tripping. The following application
examples include the suggested non fail-safe and fail-safe wiring for contactors.
•

Non fail-safe operation
You can achieve non fail-safe operation with the factory-default settings by
using BO7 NC contact. The following figure shows an example of contactor
application using the default settings of BO7.

Wiring on the rear panel

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
Apply proper tightening torque to all wire connections.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Slot B-C/D-E/F-G: CT/VT analog module

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Do not open the secondary circuit of a live CT.

•

The secondary of the line CT must be shorted before opening any
connections to it.

•

A maximum of two ring terminals can be used for each terminal on the CT/
VT module only on the lower row. Do not connect more than two ring
terminals on a single terminal.

•

For CT/VT analog module with ring-type terminal block, protect the rear
connector with the CT/VT IP20 transparent cover after cabling.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
CT/VT module can be ordered with either ring-type or pin-type terminal blocks.
The external terminal block for CT/VT module includes an integral short-circuiting
feature for CT circuits. This feature provides additional protection and allows you
to remove the terminal block from the device without causing an open-circuit
condition on the secondary side. However, you should still isolate the primary
circuit before removing the terminal block.

66

P7/EN M/02-004A

Installation

Protection and control device

Connection of CT/VT ring-type terminals
When the device is ordered with CT/VT ring-type terminals, the device is delivered
with accessories including CT/VT screws, CT/VT jumpers, IP20 cover, and barrier.
See Package contents, page 48.
The three-terminal jumper is provided to support a common connection.

General cabling operation on CT/VT ring-type terminals
The following procedure presents the general mounting and cabling operation on
the CT/VT ring-type terminals:
1. Insert and connect the terminal jumper for common connections.

P711BYA

•

•

of terminals. Do not share terminals between jumpers.

2. Fasten the terminal jumper with the provided terminal screws.

Connection of CT/VT pin-type terminals

General cabling operation on CT/VT pin-type terminals
To perform the cabling on CT/VT pin-type terminals:
1. Loosen the terminal screws.
2. (Optional) Install a three-terminal jumper on the required terminals (for
example, terminals 2, 4, and 6).

1

2

1
1

P711HHA

NOTE:
•

Use jumpers on even-numbered terminals only.

•

If you use multiple three-terminal jumpers, install each on a separate
set of terminals. Do not share terminals between jumpers.

3. Attach the cable end to the required terminal, or to any bridged terminal if a
jumper is installed.

inputs are contained in the same cable.

The electrical conductors connected to the binary inputs of PowerLogic P7
devices should run along the metal structures of the cubicle to reduce ground
loops. The conductors are contained in the same strand and, if possible twisted, to
avoid the creation of cabling loops.

NOTICE
UNINTENDED EQUIPMENT OPERATION
•

Avoid large cabling loops in the various power supplies when connecting the
binary inputs.

•

Do not short-circuit any of the galvanic insulation.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in improper operation or
equipment damage.
When the environmental and installation conditions are highly unfavorable for
PowerLogic P7, a shielded twisted pair should be used. In such cases, the cable
shielding is connected to the local ground at both ends (provided that the
installation has an equipotential bonding network).

(18) The terminal reference x refers to the first slot used by the first analog module which takes 2 slots. For example, if the first analog
module is fitted in slots B & C then x9 becomes B9.

Installation

Protection and control device

Slot Z: power supply + 2 Double Pole C/O Contact (DPCO) + WD (PSU
module)
Power supply

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
•

Protect the power supplying circuit of PowerLogic P7 against overcurrents.
(MCB recommendation - DC: 6 A; AC: 10 A)

•

For breaking capacity higher than 40 W with L/R = 40 ms, protect the binary
output contacts with an additional customer protection (RC or zener diode).

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
The power supply available for PowerLogic P7 is:
•

24...34 V DC

•

48...125 V DC

•

110...250 V DC/AC

The nominal voltage range of PowerLogic P7 is specified on the slot Z board.

NOTICE
POWER SUPPLY DAMAGE
Make sure that the nominal value of the auxiliary device voltage corresponds
with the nominal value of the auxiliary system voltage before connecting the
auxiliary voltage to PowerLogic P7.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.

Binary Outputs
The binary output module (WD + 2DPCO) is mounted in slot Z. BO7 and BO8 are
equipped with a 2-pole switching contact to control a circuit breaker. Binary output
WD is defined as a Watchdog contact. The contacts are referred to BO7 and BO8
as they are driven from the TIO board. The setting and operation of these contacts
is part of Slot Y configuration

inputs to allow supervision of trip and close circuits.

Connecting ground

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Connect the device to the ground with a nut and a washer using the
indicated stud terminals.

•

Check the equipotential grounding network and test the grounding during
installation.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
The ground connections are accessible on the rear panel of the devices. For
PowerLogic P7, there is one connector located in the bottom right corner of the
device.
Figure 39 - Ground studs on PowerLogic P7

Every PowerLogic P7 device must be connected to the local ground bar using one
M4 ground stud on the device case to prevent dangerous voltages being present
in case of a wiring fault or damage to the product. Also, it ensures that
PowerLogic P7 meets EMC claims.

NOTE: To avoid any possibility of electrolytic action between brass or copper
ground conductors and the rear panel of PowerLogic P7, precautions should
be taken to isolate them from one another. It is recommended to place a
nickel-plated or insulating washer between the conductor and PowerLogic P7
case or to use tinned ring terminals.

Rear communication ports and modules at Slot A

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Do not connect items of equipment with different ground potentials with an
RJ45 cable.

•

Do not use RJ45 cable longer than 10 m (32.8 ft) between a merging unit
and the device.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
PowerLogic P7 includes rear communication ports and modules with RJ45 or fiber
optic (optional) connections.
The ports and modules are accessible from Slot A (CPU) of the rear panel.

Port 1 at slot A

RJ45 connector with communication indicators

P711AIA

Ethernet connection

100 Mbps

Maximum cable length

100 m (328.08 ft)

Port 2 and Port 3: Ethernet port for optional SFP modules
Port 2 and Port 3 are Ethernet communication ports for the optional SFP modules.
The SFP modules can be selected as accessories when ordering the device or
purchased later and installed on site.
The SFP modules are available for copper wire, multi-mode fiber optic or singlemode fiber optic connection.
The SFP modules with RJ45 or LC connector provide RSTP (Rapid Spanning
Tree Protocol), PRP (Parallel Redundancy Protocol), HSR (High-availability
Seamless Redundancy), and Failover redundancy, which are selectable by
configuration. When two SFP modules are used, instantaneous reconfiguration of
the communication system is allowed without communication packet loss. When
only one SFP module is used, the default Failover mode should be selected which
will work with the SFP installed in either port.
Figure 42 - Example of SFP module connection
HSR
SCADA
RedBox

P711AKB

LAN-A

IED

LAN-B

IED

IED

P711ALB

Installing the optional SFP modules
The SFP modules are designed with a self-locking mechanism and are hot
swappable. After sliding all the way into position along the ports, the module locks
itself in final place. The bale-clasp latch at one end locks the SFP module in place.
Insert the SFP modules into port 2 and port 3 on slot A.

P7/EN M/02-004A

83

Protection and control device

Installation

NOTICE
IMPROPER EQUIPMENT OPERATION
•

Use only Schneider Electric approved optical transceiver components.

•

Never replace the optical transceiver components with unauthorized
manufactured parts.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.
Use optical power meters to determine the operation or signal level of the device.
If electrical-to-optical converters are used, they must have management of
character idle state capability (when the fiber optic cable interface is "Light off").
Specific care should be taken with the bending radius of the fibers, and the use of
optical shunts is not recommended as these can decrease the communication
performance of the transmission path lifetime.

Serial port: 2-wire RS485 communication port
The 2-wire RS485 communication port is located at the slot A (CPU). The RS485
connector is included in the accessory box. Install the RS485 connector on the
serial port.

RS485 communication port
PowerLogic P7 can be connected to any RS485 half duplex communication
network and can exchange the data necessary for centralized management of the
electrical installation by a SCADA. The serial RS485 ports are designed for wiring
connections with the following characteristics for each terminal.

DHMI module (reference REL70124)
Description
The DHMI module is an external module that can be connected with
PowerLogic P7 (no HMI option) through the DHMI port. When connected, the
DHMI offers the same functionality as the HMI integrated with PowerLogic P7. It
can be selected as an accessory when ordering PowerLogic P7 (no HMI option) or
purchased later as a spare and installed on site.

P711GLB

the DHMI before it can be used. Follow on-screen instructions to update the
firmware.

NOTICE
EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
Ensure that the power supply is not interrupted during the firmware update
process.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in non-functional DHMI.

Mounting

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Apply appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow safe
electrical work practices.

•

Turn off all power supplying this device and the equipment in which it is
installed before working on the device or equipment.

•

Verify that all power is off using a properly rated voltage sensing device.

•

Treat communications and IO wiring connected to multiple devices as
hazardous live until determined otherwise.

•

Do not exceed the device’s ratings for maximum limits.

•

Do not use displayed data as a substitute for proper workplace practices or
equipment maintenance.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
The DHMI module must be mounted on a panel when connected with
PowerLogic P7 (no HMI option) for long-term use. The mounting method differs
depending on the holes prepared on the panel.

P712HAA

Installing the PSU ring-type terminal block

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

NEVER work alone.

•

Apply appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow safe
electrical work practices.

•

Turn off all power supplying the protection and control device and equipment
in which it is installed before working on it.

•

Verify that all power is off using a properly rated voltage sensing device.

•

Do not use the PSU ring-type terminal block on slots other than Slot Z.

•

Protect the PSU ring-type terminal block with the IP20 cover after cabling.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
•

Apply proper tightening torque to all wire connections.

•

In case of replacement, perform the commissioning operations before using
the protection and control device.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

WARNING
UNINTENDED DEVICE OPERATION
Verify that the PSU terminal block is inserted in Slot Z and secured after
removing it for wiring or testing.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or
equipment damage.

MET148-2 temperature sensor module (reference 59641)

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
Verify that the temperature sensors are isolated from dangerous voltages.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Description
The MET148-2 temperature sensor module is an external module used for
temperature measurement with Resistance Temperature Detectors (RTDs). It is
connected to the EXT port on slot A (CPU) of PowerLogic P7. It can be selected
as an accessory when ordering the device or purchased later as spare and
installed on site. One temperature sensor module provides 8 RTD inputs.

P7/EN M/02-004A

97

Protection and control device

Installation

Figure 48 - MET148-2 temperature sensor module

P711GEA

The MET148-2 module can be used to connect 8 temperature sensors (RTDs) of
the same type:
•

Pt100, Ni100 or Ni120 type RTDs, according to parameter setting

•

3-wire temperature sensors

Connection
The MET148-2 module is connected to PowerLogic P7 from its Da port. The cable
must be shielded twisted pair and of a length no more than 3 m (10 ft).
Recommended connection cord:
•

59660: CCA770 remote module connection cord, L = 0.6 m (2 ft)

•

59661: CCA772 remote module connection cord, L = 2 m (6.6 ft)

Figure 49 - Connection of the MET148-2 module

98

P7/EN M/02-004A

Installation

Protection and control device

Connectors and terminals on the module:
•

A: Terminal block for RTDs 1 to 4

•

B: Terminal block for RTDs 5 to 8

•

Da: RJ45 connector to connect the module to PowerLogic P7

•

Dd: RJ45 connector to link up the next remote module

•

: Grounding terminal

Jumper for impedance matching with load resistor (Rc), to be set to:
•

, if the module is not the last interlinked module (default position).

•

, if the module is the last interlinked module.
NOTE: The MET148-2 module is always after IRIG-B.

Jumper used to select module number, to be set to:
•

MET1: 1st MET148-2 module, to measure temperatures T1 to T8 (default
position)

•

MET2: 2nd MET148-2 module, to measure temperatures T9 to T16.

IRIG-B module (reference REL51045)
Description
The IRIG-B module is an external module used for accurate time synchronization.
It is connected to the EXT port on the slot A (CPU). It can be selected as an
accessory when ordering the device or purchased later as spare and installed on
site.
The module provides both a modulated (MOD INPUT) and an unmodulated input
(UNMOD INPUT) and can automatically detect which input type is used by the
user. No configuration of input type is needed in PowerLogic P7.
It does not require any auxiliary supply as the module gets powered over the
connection interface.

(19) Depth is 70 mm (2.8 in) with cable connected.

P7/EN M/02-004A

99

Protection and control device

P711CAA

Connection

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Install the IRIG-B module between PowerLogic P7 and any other
accessories like the MET148-2 temperature module.

•

Do not interface any accessories between PowerLogic P7 and the IRIG-B
module.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
The IRIG-B module is connected to PowerLogic P7 from its EXT IN port. The
cable used for connection must be shielded and of a length not exceeding 3 m (10
ft).
The IRIG-B module provides an additional extension port (EXT OUT) for
connecting other accessories such as the MET148-2 module with 59660/59661
cords.
The time source is connected to the module through the modulated input, or
through the unmodulated input by connecting signal + to the IRIG-B terminal, and
signal - to the 0+ terminal.
NOTE: If one source is connected on the modulated input and another one on
the unmodulated input, the modulated signal has the priority.

CLIO module (reference REL70071)
Description
The Current Loop Input/Output (CLIO) module is an external module that provides
analog input and output capability for PowerLogic P7. It can be selected as an
accessory when ordering PowerLogic P7 or purchased later as a spare and
installed on site.

Connection

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

NEVER work alone.

•

Turn off all power supplying this equipment before working on or inside it.

•

Verify that all power is off using a properly rated voltage sensing device.

•

Connect the module to the ground with a ring terminal attached to the
ground screw.

•

Check the equipotential grounding network and test the grounding during
installation.

•

Do not interface any accessories between PowerLogic P7 and the IRIG-B
module.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
•

Apply proper tightening torque to all wire connections.

•

Never touch electronic parts.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Connecting to PowerLogic P7
Connect the CLIO module (after the IRIG-B) to PowerLogic P7 using the EXT-IN
communication port. The cable must be shielded twisted pair and of a length no
more than 3 m (10 ft).
Recommended connection cord:
•

59660: CCA770 remote module connection cord, L = 0.6 m (2 ft)

•

59661: CCA772 remote module connection cord, L = 2 m (6.6 ft)

P7/EN M/02-004A

①

PowerLogic P7

②

CLIO module

③ Connection cord

103

Protection and control device

Installation

NOTE:
1. For the last CLIO module in the loop, toggle the RC switch up to set the
module as termination of network.
2. When the MET148-2 module and the CLIO module are connected (after
the IRIG-B) to PowerLogic P7 by daisy chaining, a maximum of two
MET148-2 modules and one CLIO module can be connected. The
address of MET148-2 module can be set to MET1 or MET2 and the
address of the CLIO module is 000. If the last module in the loop is the
MET148-2 module, when it’s disconnected from PowerLogic P7, the
communication does not rebuild automatically when the physical
connection is returned. To rebuild the communication, unplug and plug
the CAN cable after the IRIG-B.

Connecting the analog inputs and outputs
Each CLIO module provides four current loop input channels and four current loop
output channels.

Connecting to the power supply
Connect the module to an external power supply (24...250 V DC, 100...250 V AC).
Refer to Wiring specification, page 78 for detailed information on wiring the screwtype terminals.

Connecting the functional ground
Connect the module to the ground with a ring lug terminal attached to the ground
screw. Use cable with cross-section of 2.5...6 mm2 (AWG 12...10) and fitted with a
4 mm (0.16 in) ring lug at the cable end. The maximum cable length is 2 m (6.5 ft.).
Tighten the ground screw with tightening torque of 1...1.2 N·m (8.85...10.62 lb-in).

Protection and control device

Commissioning

Commissioning
Principles
DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

NEVER work alone.

•

Only qualified personnel can commission this equipment. Such work must
be performed only after reading this entire set of instructions.

•

Obey all existing safety instructions when commissioning and maintaining
high-voltage equipment.

•

Apply appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow safe
electrical work practices.

•

Do not choose lower PPE while working on energized equipment.

•

Beware of potential hazardous voltages from open circuited current
transformers, any voltage transformers and any capacitors.

•

Before energizing verify that the protection device and other devices are
connected to a protective ground in accordance with the instructions
provided.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
NOTE: See NFPA 70E, CSA Z462 or national equivalent.

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
Apply proper tightening torque to all wire connections.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

Never open the secondary circuit of a live CT.

•

The secondary of the line CT must be shorted before opening any
connections to it.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

WARNING
UNINTENDED EQUIPMENT OPERATION
Do not energize the primary circuit before the protection device is properly
configured.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or
equipment damage.
PowerLogic P7 devices are tested prior to commissioning, with the aim of
maximizing availability and minimizing the risk of malfunctioning of the device and
all connected accessories.
PowerLogic P7 devices are fully numerical in their design and implementation of
the protection functions and non-protection functions in the firmware. The devices
use a high degree of self-checking and give an alarm. Therefore, the

106

P7/EN M/02-004A

Commissioning

Protection and control device

commissioning tests do not need to be as extensive as with non-numeric
electronic or electro-mechanical relays.
To commission PowerLogic P7 devices, it is necessary to verify that the hardware
is functioning correctly and the application function settings have been applied as
expected. To confirm that the device is operating correctly once the settings have
been applied, it is necessary to perform basic functional tests on each active
protection element one by one.
The main tasks for the commissioning test are as follows:
•

Device check

•

Secondary injection test

•

Primary injection test (Optional)

•

Final check

Testing tools and equipment
The following tools and equipment are needed for the commissioning:
•

•

AC current and voltage injection sources
◦

For secondary injection test, on conventional CTs and VTs, to check the
device functions, the injection source should be at such a rating that the
current is adjustable up to at least 5 Inom (Inom = 1 A or 5 A) and the
voltage is adjustable up to at least 110 V.

◦

If the primary injection test is necessary to check the CT and VT primary
connection and polarity, the injection source should be at such a rating
that the minimum current at the CT secondary is larger than 20 mA (2% of
the nominal current) and the minimum voltage at the secondary is larger
than 750 mV (1% of the nominal voltage).

DC voltage source
Adjustable from 24 to 250 V DC, for adaptation to the voltage level of the logic
input being used and tested.

•

Multimeters
◦

With suitable AC current range, and AC/DC voltage ranges

◦

Phase angle meter

•

A portable PC with PowerLogic Engineering Suite installed

•

USB cable with mini-USB type B interface or RJ45 Ethernet cable

•

For CT/VT, test block and test plug for secondary injection testing
◦

Plug with cord to match the current test block installed

◦

Plug with cord to match the voltage test block installed

Device check
Checking with the device de-energized
The following group of tests should be carried out without the auxiliary power
supply applied to PowerLogic P7 and with the trip circuit isolated. The current and
voltage transformer connections must be isolated from the device for these
checks. It is suggested to apply the test block to isolate the primary system, as
shown in Typical test block connection diagram , page 108.

Never open-circuit the secondary circuit of a current transformer.

•

Before the test plug is inserted into the test block, make sure the sockets in
the test plug which correspond to the current transformer secondary
windings are linked.

•

Do not remove the CT/VT IP20 transparent cover during testing.

•

After commissioning, verify that the CT/VT IP20 transparent cover is
properly fitted and secured with the screw.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Before inserting the test plug, refer to the scheme diagram. For example, the test
block may be associated with protection current transformer circuits. If
PowerLogic P7 is installed in the switchgear, the test block is not always provided.
In the application case without a test block, it is mandatory to isolate the voltage
transformer supply to PowerLogic P7 using the panel links or connecting blocks,
and to short-circuit them on substation side and disconnect the line current
transformers from the device terminals. Where means of isolating the auxiliary
power supply and trip circuit (such as isolation links, fuses and MCB) are
provided, these should be used. If this is impossible, the wiring to these circuits
must be disconnected and the exposed ends suitably terminated.

Visual inspection
The visual inspection should include the following aspects:

108

•

Check the rating information on PowerLogic P7.

•

Check that PowerLogic P7 being tested is correct for the protected objective.

•

Check that the circuit reference and system details are entered onto the
setting record sheet.

•

Carefully examine PowerLogic P7 to see that no physical damage has
occurred since installation.

P7/EN M/02-004A

Commissioning

Protection and control device

•

Check that the case grounding connections, at the rear of the protection and
control device case, are used to connect the protection and control device to
a local ground bar using an adequate conductor (see Connecting ground,
page 79).

•

Check that the external wiring is correct to the relevant device diagram and
wiring scheme. Check that the phase rotation appears as expected.

•

Check the connections against the wiring diagram if a test block is provided.

External wiring

Auxiliary power supply
PowerLogic P7 can be operated from either a DC or AC auxiliary power supply.
Without energizing the device, measure the auxiliary power supply to help ensure
it is within the operating range.
The rated voltage is:
•

24...34 V DC

•

48...125 V DC

•

110...250 V DC/AC

The incoming voltage must be within the operating range specified as:
•

19.2...41 V DC

•

38.4...150 V DC

•

88...300 V DC/ 80.3...288 V AC

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
The power supply must be turned off for at least 5 seconds before power supply
module is removed.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

NOTICE
POWER SUPPLY DAMAGE
Make sure that the nominal value of the auxiliary device voltage corresponds
with the nominal value of the auxiliary system voltage before connecting the
auxiliary voltage to PowerLogic P7.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.

Watchdog
Using a continuity tester, check that the watchdog contacts (WD of slot Z) are in
the states shown in the connection diagram for a de-energized device.

Before performing hardware and software tests:
•

Isolate the current and voltage transformer connections from the protection
device.

•

Isolate the trip circuit.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
The following group of tests verify that PowerLogic P7 hardware and software is
functioning correctly and should be carried out with the auxiliary power supply
applied to the device.

Energizing the device
•

Switch on the auxiliary power supply.

•

Check that PowerLogic P7 performs the following initialization sequence:
1. All four fixed LEDs are illuminated. The boot screen displays and the

is on.

Watchdog
Use a continuity tester to verify that the Watchdog contact is linked to the
successful start-up of the relay.

Binary inputs
This test verifies that all binary inputs (opto-isolated) of PowerLogic P7 are
functioning correctly. Check the terminal configuration schemes in PowerLogic P7
rear panel, page 58 for terminal numbers. Check the polarity and connect the
external 24V DC/48V DC/110V DC/220V DC/220V AC supply voltage to the
appropriate terminals for the input being tested, see Configuring binary inputs
(LPDI), page 175 for settable voltage values. Energize the opto-isolated input one
by one.
NOTE: The external power supply is used for this test, but only after
confirming that it is suitably rated, with the variation less than 20%.

110

P7/EN M/02-004A

Commissioning

Protection and control device

The status of each opto-isolated input can be viewed from the HMI under >
General > Device config > Slot # > BI status. # refers to the code of the slot (for
example, slot G). The status of each BI should be true.

Binary outputs
This test checks that all the output contact relays are functioning correctly using
the Test-blocked mode.
Connect a continuity tester across the terminals corresponding to output relay as
shown in the relevant terminal configuration schemes in PowerLogic P7 rear
panel, page 58.
To operate the output relay BO1 in Slot Y:
1. From the HMI, expand > Control > Device mode and select Test-blocked
from the Mode drop-down to set the physical device (LD0) to Test-blocked
mode.
2. Expand > General > Device config > Slot Y+Z and select the BO status
tab to force the individual relays.
Repeat the test for the rest of the output relays, and then return PowerLogic P7 to
service by setting the IED mode back to On.
NOTE:
•

Ensure that the thermal ratings of anything connected to the output relays
during the contact test procedure are not exceeded by the over-operated
output contact relays. Keep the time between application and removal of
contact test to a minimum.

•

Slot Z contacts BO7 and BO8 are controlled by the TIO module in Slot Y.

Model number and firmware version
To view the model number and the firmware version:
1. From the HMI, expand

> General > System info > Device info.

2. Check the Product code and Firmware version information displayed.

Slot health
If the
LED is on, it indicates a problem with the device firmware or hardware.
The hardware health can be checked from the HMI under > General > Device
config > Slot # > Slot info.
Six health states are defined for each slot as follows. Ensure that the health state
shows OK; otherwise, perform troubleshooting until the health state is OK.
•

Not fitted

•

Mismatch

•

Absent

•

OK

•

Warning

•

Alarm
NOTE: If all board health states are OK or Not fitted, and the
LED is on,
check the device firmware under > General > Device config > Slot A >
Version info > Firmware info.

P7/EN M/02-004A

111

Protection and control device

Commissioning

Date and time
The date and time can be synchronized with three different external clock sources,
PTP (IEEE 1588), IRIG-B or SNTP. The clock can also be set from the HMI under
> General > System clock > Date and time if not synchronized to an external
time source. See Configuring time synchronization, page 168.

Communication ports
PowerLogic P7 supports both serial and Ethernet communication simultaneously.

Serial communication protocols
The serial port can be configured to use one of the following protocols:
•

Modbus server

•

DNP3

•

IEC 60870-5-103

When multiple protocols are enabled on the serial port, only one protocol can be
active at a time. The active protocol is determined by a fixed priority rule:
•

Modbus server has the highest priority. If Modbus server is enabled together
with any other protocol, Modbus server will be active and the others will be
disabled.

•

If Modbus server is not enabled but DNP3 and IEC 60870-5-103 are both
enabled, DNP3 will be active.

•

IEC 60870-5-103 will only be active if it is the only protocol enabled.

The serial port parameters, for example, baud rate, parity can be configured
according to the selected protocol.

Ethernet communication protocols
The IED supports the following protocols for Ethernet-based data exchange:
•

IEC 61850

•

DNP3

•

Modbus server

All Ethernet protocols can be enabled at the same time. For each active protocol,
a dedicated IP address must be configured to establish communication with
clients.

Secondary injection test
The secondary injection test is to check the device analog input signal processing
and basic protection functions is working with the application-specific settings.
In the application case with test block, the connection diagram and the test plug
mounted is illustrated in the Typical secondary injection test connection diagram
with test block, page 113.
