Loose of communication during
operation.

Firmware update
WARNING
UNINTENDED DEVICE OPERATION
Take the device out of service before upgrading or downgrading the firmware.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or
equipment damage.

NOTICE
LOSS OF DATA
Save configuration files, disturbance records and events before upgrading or
downgrading the firmware.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in permanent loss of data.

P7/EN M/02-004A

611

Protection and control device

Maintenance

NOTICE
EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
Ensure that the power supply is not interrupted during the firmware upgrade or
downgrade process.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in non-functional device.

NOTICE
UNINTENDED EQUIPMENT OPERATION AND NUISANCE TRIPPING
After writing new firmware, configurations or settings to a protection and control
device, perform a test to verify that the protection and control device operates
correctly with the updated parameters.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in unwanted shutdown of
the electrical installation.
PowerLogic P7 allows upgrading or downgrading the firmware of the device with
PowerLogic Engineering Suite.
Reasons for upgrading the firmware:
•

New functionality

•

Security patches

•

Correction of product behavior

Reasons for downgrading the firmware:
•

Device homologation

•

Device configuration compatibility

Updating the firmware
Prerequisites:
•

The device is not in service status.

•

The PC is connected to your device. See Connection modes, page 158.

•

Device credentials. See Default password setting, page 146.

•

Firmware package (.zip).
NOTE: Visit the Schneider Electric website (www.se.com) to get the
firmware package.

To install the firmware package with PowerLogic Engineering Suite:
1. From the Project page, select

> Update device firmware.

2. Select the firmware package (.zip) from your PC.
3. Configure the connection settings from the prompt, and select Connect.
4. If prompted, enter device credentials, and select Authenticate.
5. Confirm the prompted Information comparison .
– Select OK to proceed. Go to Step 6.
– Select Disconnect to exit.
NOTE: Connection is not allowed for unmatched device model
(incompatible order code/data model).

612

P7/EN M/02-004A

Maintenance

Protection and control device

6. After a successful connection, a prompt will remind you to save the
configuration.
– Select Ignore and continue to start sending the firmware package to the
device. Go to Step 7.
– Select Save to exit and switch to synchronization process. See
Synchronizing parameters, page 162.
7. When the prompt indicates Firmware files sent, select Next to proceed.
Firmware update starts automatically on the device.
NOTE: The device will reboot during this process. Do not interrupt the
power supply of the device during this period.
8. When the prompt indicates device availability, confirm the current version, and
select Close to complete the process.
After a successful firmware update, the HMI of the rebooted device will indicate
Default mode, which means the device is written with the new firmware with
default settings.
Post-update steps:
1. Verify the firmware version from the HMI (

> General > System info).

2. Restore the settings from the saved configuration files or by configuration
conversion.
For more details, see Converting device configuration, page 613.
3. Perform functional tests after firmware update.

Converting device configuration
PowerLogic P7 allows converting the existing data model configuration from
current firmware version to a later firmware version with PowerLogic Engineering
Suite.
Prerequisites:
•

PowerLogic Engineering Suite is logged in.

•

A data model based on the target firmware version is available on
PowerLogic Engineering Suite.

To convert the data model configuration to a different firmware version:
1. From project page, select

> Convert device configuration.

2. Select the target firmware version from drop down, and select Convert.
If the desired firmware version is not in the drop down list, upload the data
model from > Modules & Licenses > Data Models.
3. When the prompt indicates Operation completed, select Close to complete
the process.
The data model configuration is successfully converted to the target firmware
version.
If the prompt indicates Operation failed instead, the configuration conversion is
failed. Select Show logs for details.

License update
The main application and application level of the device can be changed by
updating the license file with PowerLogic Engineering Suite. Depending on the
ordered device option, the license update can be performed via the Mini-USB/
DHMI port or Port 1/2/3.

P7/EN M/02-004A

613

Protection and control device

Maintenance

NOTE:
•

Higher level devices cannot be downgraded to a lower level (e.g. L1 to
L0).

•

Contact your local Schneider Electric sales representative to get the
license file or firmware package.

Updating device license to a higher level
This procedure applies when the device license is updated to a higher level, but
the main application is unchanged (e.g. L0 Motor to L1 Motor).
NOTE: The device configuration is retained in this procedure.
Prerequisites:
•

The device is not in service.

•

A new license file. The serial number of this license file must match the
device.

•

A PowerLogic Engineering Suite account with the Installer or Application
administrator role.

•

A device account with the ENGINEER role.

•

If Port 1/2/3 are used, the web server logical port needs to be enabled.

To update the device license:
1. Connect the device to PowerLogic Engineering Suite with accounts listed in
prerequisites.
2. In device view, click > Update device license.
3. In the pop-up window, import the new license file.
4. After the prompt Check Completed appears, click Next.
5. Confirm the update summary, and click Send.
The new license file will be written into the device. When this process finishes,
the device will reboot .
NOTE: When updating the license for devices with V02.002.002 or earlier
firmware, PowerLogic Engineering Suite will show License Update Failed
in Step 5, and the device will not automatically reboot.
Manually reboot the device to finish the license update.

Updating device license to another application

NOTICE
LOSS OF DATA
Save configuration files, disturbance records and events before updating the
license.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in permanent loss of data.
This procedure applies when the device license is updated to any of the following:
•

Another application (e.g. L0 Motor to L0 Generator)

•

Another application and a higher level (e.g. L0 Motor to L1 Generator)
NOTE: The device will load the default configuration of the target application
in this procedure.

Prerequisites:
•

614

The device is not in service.

P7/EN M/02-004A

Maintenance

Protection and control device

•

A new license file. The serial number of this license file must match the
device.

•

A firmware package that is the same version as currently installed on the
device.

•

A PowerLogic Engineering Suite account with the Installer or Application
administrator role.

•

A device account with the ENGINEER role.

•

If Port 1/2/3 are used, the web server logical port needs to be enabled.

To update the device license:
1. Connect the device to PowerLogic Engineering Suite with accounts listed in
prerequisites.
2. In device view, click > Update device license.
3. In the pop-up window, import the new license file.
4. After the prompt Check Completed appears, click Next.
5. Import the firmware package.
6. After the prompt Check Completed appears, click Next.
7. Confirm the update summary, and click Send.
The new license file and default configuration will be written into the device.
When this process finishes, the device will reboot.
NOTE: When updating the license for devices with V02.002.002 or earlier
firmware, PowerLogic Engineering Suite will show License Update Failed
in Step 7, and the device will not automatically reboot.
Manually reboot the device to finish the license update.
After the update, the device will have the new application with default settings
and will be disconnected from the current PowerLogic Engineering Suite
device, which is no longer compatible with the new application.
A compatible configuration should then be loaded to the device.

Replacing the modules
Identification of the modules
PowerLogic P7 is a modular protection and control device. The modules fitted on
all slots can be replaced individually.
To access the identification of the modules:
•

From the HMI of PowerLogic P7, expand > General > Device config to
locate to the desired slot, navigate to Version info or Slot info.

•

From PowerLogic Engineering Suite, expand Device configuration to locate
to the desired slot, navigate to Basic info.
NOTE: Slot A represents the CPU module.

P7/EN M/02-004A

615

Protection and control device

Maintenance

Replacing the CPU module

CAUTION
RISK OF SETTING CHANGES ERASED
•

Never plug in or draw out the module while PowerLogic P7 is in service.

•

Verify that the module is secured with retaining screws.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in injury or equipment
damage.
To access the identification of the CPU module, see Identification of the modules,
page 615 for more information.

Diagnosis for replacement
Check the following items on the device:
•

The status of the watchdog relay on slot Z.

•

The status of

and

LED indicators on the front panel.

•

The status of ! and

LED indicators on the front panel.

•

Tap

to review the configurable virtual LED status on HMI.

•

Tap

to review the Alarm list page on HMI, if shown.

•

Tap

to review the Device maintenance page on HMI, if shown.

Preliminary operations
1. Power off PowerLogic P7.
2. Check if there is enough space (> 35 cm or 13.78 in) to extract the module. If
there is not enough space, remove the device from its mounting.
3. Check if the external signals are disconnected from the device.
4. Remove the wires, external modules, SFP modules and serial port terminal
block connected to CPU module (see Changing a SFP module, page 621 for
removing SFP modules).

P7/EN M/02-004A

2. Pull out the CPU module to detach it from the device with the help of the
retaining screws.

P711CCA

Installation
1. Visually check the cleanliness and state of the place where the CPU module
is to be installed before replacing the CPU module.
2. Insert the CPU module into the device case.

the following steps:
1. Insert wires, external modules, SFP modules and serial port terminal block
into location (see Changing a SFP module, page 621 for installing SFP
modules).
2. Power on PowerLogic P7.
3. Verify that the module is detected.
4. Verify that the
on the HMI.

LED is on, the

LED is off, and the

icon is not shown

Functional tests should be performed after the replacement. See Commissioning,
page 106.

Replacing other modules

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH
•

NEVER work alone.

•

Apply appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow safe
electrical work practices.

•

Turn off all power supplying this equipment before working on or inside it.

•

Verify that all power is off using a properly rated voltage sensing device.

•

Protect the ring-type terminal blocks with the appropriate IP20 cover after
cabling.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
NOTE: Consider all sources of power when turning off the power supply,
including the possibility of backfeeding.

DANGER
FIRE HAZARD
•

Apply proper tightening torque to all wire connections.

•

Disconnect the power supply before removing or replacing a module.

•

Never touch electronics parts.

•

In case of module replacement, perform the commissioning operations
before using the protection and control device.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

618

P7/EN M/02-004A

Maintenance

Protection and control device

WARNING
UNINTENDED DEVICE OPERATION
•

Verify that the terminal blocks are restored into the correct location after
removing them for wiring or testing.

•

Verify that the modules are secured with retaining screws.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or
equipment damage.

CAUTION
RISK OF SETTING CHANGES ERASED
•

Never plug in or draw out the module while PowerLogic P7 is in service.

•

Verify that the module is secured with retaining screws.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in injury or equipment
damage.

CAUTION
CUTS AND PHYSICAL IMPACT
Wear gloves when handling modules during replacement.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in injury or equipment
damage.

NOTICE
EQUIPMENT DAMAGE DUE TO ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE (ESD)
Wear an ESD wrist strap to keep grounded before replacing the board.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.

NOTICE
EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
During the first power-on after the replacement, ensure that the power supply of
the device is uninterrupted for at least 5 minutes to allow system update and
reboot.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in a non-functional device.

Returning for expert assessment
To return PowerLogic P7 for expert assessment, use the original packaging if
possible.
PowerLogic P7 must be returned accompanied by its settings sheet and the
following information:
•

Name and address of the initiator

•

PowerLogic P7 type and serial number

•

Date of the incident

•

Detailed description of the incident

•

LED status and message displayed at the time of the incident

•

List of stored events

•

Repair and Modification Authorization (RMA) form which is from the country
organization

End of life
At end of life of PowerLogic P7, Schneider Electric's Environmental Data Program
helps decrease the amount of waste and allow recovery of product components
and materials. Refer to the Product Environmental Profile and the End of Life
instructions available on the Schneider Electric website: www.se.com. The
instructions can also be accessed from the PowerLogic P7 landing page by
scanning the product QR code, see Equipment identification, page 49.
Dispose the device according to the legislation of the country when
decommissioning the device. Proper disposal helps prevent potential disclosure of
data contained in the device that was not removed.

NOTICE
UNAUTHORIZED OR UNINTENDED ACCESS TO CONFIDENTIAL DATA
•

Verify that all data from the device was saved and erased.

•

Perform a factory reset to recover the device's default configuration.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in permanent loss of data.

LED test via reset button long press added.
Block setting group change added.
HMI QR code added.
Web HMI function added.
Mimic SLD scaling and additional equipment added. Grid
size reduced.
Identified and fixed

without main time source and backup time source.
TRxPDIF phase correction is incorrect for ACB rotation.
SR gate resetting output occurred on input change.

Test/Blocked.
Only SMV backwards compatible Invalid quality (01) was
supported.

than actual performance.

in published GOOSE.
Matrix_BO2 status (True/False) was incorrectly linked to
BO1 status.
